The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

2.ï¿½ Definitions.ï¿½

Inï¿½ thisï¿½ Act,ï¿½ unlessï¿½ theï¿½ contextï¿½ otherwise requires,--

  1. "Chief  Election Commissioner"  means the Chief Election Commissioner appointed under article 324 of the Constitution;

  2. 3*[`"Electionï¿½ Commission" means the Election Commission referred to in article 324 of the Constitution;'.]

  3. 3*["Electionï¿½ Commissioner" meansï¿½ anyï¿½ otherï¿½ Election Commissioner appointed under article 324 of the Constitution.

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

3. Salary.ï¿½

  1. There  shall  be  paid  to  the  Chief  Election Commissioner a  salary which  is equal to the salary of a Judge of the  Supreme Court.

  2. There  shall be  paid to  an Election  Commissioner a  salary  which is equal to the salary of a Judge of a High Court:

Provided thatï¿½ if aï¿½ person who,ï¿½ immediately beforeï¿½ the date of assuming office as the Chief Election Commissioner or, as the case may be, an Election Commissioner, was in receipt of, or, being eligible so to do,ï¿½ had electionï¿½ to draw,ï¿½ a pensionï¿½ (other than a disability or wound pension) in respect of any previous service under the Government of the Union or under the Government of a State, his salary in respect of service as the

  1. Subs by Act 4 of 1994, S.2 (w.e.f 1-10-1993 )

  2. Subs by S.3 ibid (w.e.f. 1-10-1993)

  3. Re-lettered and ins. by S.4, ibid (w.e.f 1-10-1993 )

  4. Omitted and ins by S.5, ibid (w.e.f 1-10-1993 ) 27 Chief Electionï¿½ Commissioner or,ï¿½ as theï¿½ caseï¿½ mayï¿½ be,ï¿½ anï¿½ Election Commissioner shall be reduced--

  1. by the amount of that pension; and

  2. if  he had, before assuming office, received, in lieu of a portion  of the  pension due to him in respect of such previous service, the  commuted value  thereof,  by  the  amount  of  that portion of the pension.

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

4. Term of office.

The Chief Election Commissioner or an Election Commissioner shallï¿½ hold officeï¿½ for a term of six years from the date on which he assumes his office:

1*["Provided thatï¿½ where theï¿½ Chief Electionï¿½ Commissioner orï¿½ an Election Commissionerï¿½ attains theï¿½ age of sixty-five years before the expiry ofï¿½ the saidï¿½ term ofï¿½ six years, he shall vacate his office on the date on which he attains the said age:".]

Provided furtherï¿½ that theï¿½ Chiefï¿½ Electionï¿½ Commissionerï¿½ orï¿½ an Election Commissionerï¿½ may, atï¿½ any time,ï¿½ by writingï¿½ under hisï¿½ hand addressed to the President, resign his office. Explanation.ï¿½

For theï¿½ purpose ofï¿½ this section,ï¿½ the term of six years inï¿½ respect ofï¿½ the Chiefï¿½ Election Commissionerï¿½ or an Election Commissioner holdingï¿½ office immediatelyï¿½ before theï¿½ commencementï¿½ of this Act,ï¿½ shall beï¿½ computed fromï¿½ the dateï¿½ on whichï¿½ he had assumed office.

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

5. Leave.ï¿½

  1. Aï¿½ person who,ï¿½ immediatelyï¿½ beforeï¿½ theï¿½ dateï¿½ of assuming officeï¿½ as theï¿½ Chief Electionï¿½ Commissioner orï¿½ anï¿½ Election Commissioner, wasï¿½ in serviceï¿½ of Government may be granted during his tenure ofï¿½ office butï¿½ not thereafter, leave inï¿½ accordance withï¿½ the rules forï¿½ the timeï¿½ being applicableï¿½ toï¿½ theï¿½ Serviceï¿½ toï¿½ whichï¿½ he belonged beforeï¿½ such dateï¿½ and heï¿½ shall be entitled to carry forward theï¿½ amountï¿½ï¿½ ofï¿½ leaveï¿½ï¿½ standingï¿½ atï¿½ï¿½ hisï¿½ creditï¿½ï¿½ onï¿½ suchï¿½ date, ï¿½notwithstanding anything contained in section 6.

  2. Any  other person  who is  appointed as  the  Chief  Election  Commissioner or  an Election  Commissioner may  be  granted  leave  in  accordance with  such rules  as are for the time being applicable to a  member of the Indian Administrative Service.

  3. The  power to  grant or  refuse leave  to the  Chief Election  Commissioner or  an Election  Commissioner and  to revoke  or  curtail leave granted to him, shall vest in the President.

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

6. Pensionï¿½ payable toï¿½ Election Commissioners.

  1. A person who, immediately beforeï¿½ the dateï¿½ of assuming office as the Chief Election Commissioner or an Election Commissioner was in service of Government, shall beï¿½ deemed toï¿½ have retired from service on the date on which he enters uponï¿½ office asï¿½ the Chief Election Commissioner or an Election Commissionerï¿½ butï¿½ï¿½ hisï¿½ subsequentï¿½ serviceï¿½ asï¿½ theï¿½ Chiefï¿½ Election Commissionerï¿½ orï¿½ï¿½ anï¿½ Electionï¿½ Commissionerï¿½ shallï¿½ beï¿½ reckonedï¿½ as continuing approvedï¿½ service countingï¿½ for pension in Service to which he belonged. 1 Subs by Act 4 of 1994, S.6 (w.e.f 1-10-1993)

  2. Where  the Chief Election Commissioner demits office [whether in any  manner specified  in sub-section  (4) or  by resignation],  he shall, on such demission be entitled to--

  1. aï¿½ pension whichï¿½ is equalï¿½ to theï¿½ pension payable to a Judge ofï¿½ the Supremeï¿½ Court in accordance with the provisions of Part IIIï¿½ of the Schedule to the Supreme Court Judges (Conditions of Service)ï¿½ Act, 1958,ï¿½ (41 ofï¿½ 1958.) asï¿½ amended fromï¿½ time to time; and

  2. such  pension (including commutation of pension), family pension and  gratuity as are admissible to a Judge of the Supreme Court under  the said  Act and  the  rules  made  there under,  as amended from time to time.

  1. 1*Except  where  the  Chief  Election  Commissioner  or  an  Election Commissioner  demits  office  by  resignation,  he  shall  be deemed, for  the purpose  of this Act, to have demitted his office if, and only if,--

  1. he has completed the term of office specified in section 4, or

  2. he has attained the age of sixty-five years, 1*xxx or

  3. his  demission of  office is  medically certified  to be necessitated by ill-health.

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

7. Rightï¿½ to subscribeï¿½ to Generalï¿½ Provident Fund.

Every person holding officeï¿½ as theï¿½ Chief Electionï¿½ Commissionerï¿½ orï¿½ anï¿½ Election Commissioner shallï¿½ be entitledï¿½ to subscribe to the General Provident Fund (Central Services).

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

8. Otherï¿½ conditions ofï¿½ service.

Saveï¿½ as otherwiseï¿½ provided in this Act,ï¿½ the conditions of service relating to travelling allowance, provision of rent-free residence and exemption from payment of income- tax onï¿½ the valueï¿½ of such rent-free residence, conveyance facilities, sumptuary allowance,ï¿½ medical facilitiesï¿½ and such other conditions of service asï¿½ are, 2*["forï¿½ the time being, applicable to a Judge of the Supreme Court under Chapter IV of the Supreme Court Judges (Conditions of Service)ï¿½ Act, 1958ï¿½ (41 ofï¿½ 1958) andï¿½ the rulesï¿½ made there under, shall, soï¿½ far as may be, apply to the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners"]

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

9. Transactionï¿½ of businessï¿½ of Election Commission.

The business of theï¿½ Election Commission shall be transacted in accordance with the provisions of this Act.

The Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Conditions of Service) Act, 1991

10. Disposal of business by Election Commission.

  1. The Election Commission may,  by unanimous  decision, regulate  the  procedure  for  transaction of its business as also allocation of its business amongst  the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners.

  2. Save  as provided  in sub-section  (1), all  business of  the Election  Commission   shall,  as   far  as  possible,  be  transacted unanimously.

  3. Subject  to the  provisions of  sub-section (2), if the Chief Election Commissioner  and  other  Election  Commissioners  differ  in opinion on  any matter,  such matter shall be decided according to the opinion of the majority."]

  2. Subs by S.8, ibid (w.e.f 1-10-1993)
